report.recommendation: SKIP clustering — sub-hourly loads make clustering counterproductive
report.rationale: This table runs 41.8 times per day (sub-hourly) as a full-refresh table materialization. Each load rebuilds the table entirely, destroying any clustering applied. Automatic re-clustering would run continuously but never catch up, wasting credits with no performance benefit.

expected_improvement.estimated_query_speedup: None — clustering credits would be wasted

alternative_recommendations: Consider switching to incremental materialization to reduce load cost
If load frequency can be reduced to daily, re-evaluate clustering
Consider SOS (Search Optimization Service) for TRANSACTION_ID point lookups — SOS is load-frequency-agnostic

output.implementation_sql: -- No clustering changes recommended. Table loads sub-hourly.
caveats: Sub-hourly full-refresh loads destroy clustering on every run
Automatic re-clustering would consume credits with zero net benefit
If load schedule changes to daily, clustering should be re-evaluated
SOS for point lookups is unaffected by load frequency — consider separately

decision_log.decision: SKIP — sub-hourly load frequency makes clustering counterproductive
decision_log.rationale: 41.8 runs/day as full-refresh table. Clustering would be destroyed on every load. Credits wasted.

alternatives_considered.reason: Would be undone every ~35 minutes — net negative ROI
No ambiguity — sub-hourly full-refresh is a clear SKIP
